## Slide 3: Unit Economics (FY2025A)

The core marketplace engine is int

act at the top line, but per-order economics are compressing:

- **GMV:**
FY2025 GMV reached SAR 7,245.2m (+10.8% YoY) on a take rate of 15
.4% and an average order value of SAR 64.90.

events.quote:
Company Jahez
Sector Quick-commerce / food delivery
Stage Analysis complete (in Investment Committee review)
Origin Market screen — SAHMK/Argaam screen · 2026-07-08
Analyst Ali
FY2025 GMV reached

events.text: - **Contribution proxy:**
Gross
margin, the closest disclosed proxy for contribution margin per order, fell to 22.8% (FY2024A: 24.4%).

events.quote:
Gross margin — the closest disclosed
proxy for contribution margin per order — fell to 22.8% (FY2024A: 24.4%), and adjusted EBITDA margin
contracted to 8.3% (FY2024A: 11.3%).

events.text: - **Operating leverage:**
Adjusted EBITDA margin contracted to 8.3% (FY2024A: 11

events.quote:
Gross margin — the closest disclosed
proxy for contribution margin per order — fell to 22.8% (FY2024A: 24.4%), and adjusted EBITDA margin
contracted to 8.3% (FY2024A: 11.3%).

events.text: **Read:** Top-line GMV growth of ~11% is being offset by a ~160bp
gross-margin erosion and a ~300bp adjusted-EBITDA contraction, consistent with take-rate pressure r

ather than volume loss.

---

## Slide 4: Profitability & the One-Offs

- **Net income:**
Net income compressed 61.2% YoY to SAR 73.0m, driven by SAR 55.4m of Q4-2025 one-off items (Red Color impairment,
Marn goodwill impairment, Others-segment ECL, Snoonu deal costs) layered on take
-rate pressure from the Keeta-led price war.

events.quote:
Company Jahez
Sector Quick-commerce / food delivery
Stage Analysis complete (in Investment Committee review)
Origin Market screen — SAHMK/Argaam screen · 2026-07-08
Analyst Ali
FY2025 GMV reached

events.text: - **Recent trend:**
Q
1-2026 printed a SAR 9.2m net loss.

events.quote: Q1-2026 printed a SAR 9.2m net loss.

events.text: **Judgment (analyst):** The S
AR 55.4m of Q4 items are flagged as non-recurring, but the Q1-2026 loss indicates the margin p
ressure is ongoing and not fully one-off. What would change this flag: two consecutive quarters of take-rate st

abilization at or above 15.4% and a return to positive net income.

---

## Slide 5: Balance Sheet
The balance sheet carries SAR 16.6m of net cash: SAR 510.7m of cash and equivalents, less SAR 161.2m of debt and
SAR 332.9m of IFRS-16 lease liabilities.

events.quote:
The balance sheet carries
SAR 16.6m of net cash — SAR 510.7m of cash and equivalents, less SAR 161.2m of debt and SAR
332.9m of IFRS-16 lease liabilities.

events.text: **Read:** Net cash position is thin at SAR 16.6m;
the SAR 510.7m gross cash cushion is largely absorbed once debt and l

ease liabilities are netted. Liquidity is adequate but does not provide a large buffer for a sustained price war.

---

## Slide 6: Val
uation Methodology
Methodology: a discounted cash flow (FCFF) model, discounted at a CAPM-derived WAC
C of 13.31% (4.6% risk-free + 1.82x comp-set beta at a 5.0% equity risk premium, for a
13.7% cost of equity) with a 3.0% Gordon terminal growth rate, cross-checked against trading-comps tri
angulation (EV/Revenue, EV/EBITDA and P/E across Talabat, DoorDash and Del
ivery Hero).

events.quote:
Methodology: a discounted cash flow (FCFF) model, discounted at a CAPM-derived
WACC of 13.31% (4.6% risk-free + 1.82x comp-set beta at a 5.0% equity risk premium, for a 13.7% cost
of equity) with a

events.text: **Read:** A 13.31% WACC against a 3.0% terminal growth gives a ~10
.3-point spread, a conservative terminal setup. The 1.82x beta reflects the cyclic

ality and competitive intensity of the quick-commerce comp set.

---
